merged_events.event_description: Diplomatic relations and the American Legation in Port-au-Prince were established when Commissioner and Consul General Benjamin F. Whidden presented his credentials to the Government of Haiti.

evidence.quote: Diplomatic relations and the American Legation in Port-au-Prince were established on October 1, 1862, when Commissioner and Consul General Benjamin F. Whidden presented his credentials to the Government of the Republic of Haiti.

merged_events.event_description: The American Legation in Haiti was elevated to Embassy status following a joint announcement between the United States and seven American Republics, including Haiti.

evidence.quote: Following a joint announcement on March 23, 1943, between the United States and seven American Republics that included Haiti, the Legations in the respective nations and the United States were raised to the status of Embassy.

merged_events.event_description: Mission elevated from Commissioner and Consul General to Minister Resident/Consul General, reflecting a formal upgrade in the grade of the chief of mission.

evidence.quote: Henry Everard Peck, Commissioner and Consul General. Appointed: 1865-03-14. Credentials presented: 1865-09-02. Promoted to Minister Resident/Consul General 1866-10-11.

merged_events.event_description: Mission grade elevated to Envoy Extraordinary and Minister Plenipotentiary with the presentation of credentials by William Frank Powell.

evidence.quote: William Frank Powell, Envoy Extraordinary and Minister Plenipotentiary. Appointed: 1897-06-17. Credentials presented: 1897-08-20. Left post about 1905-11-30.

merged_events.event_description: Legation elevated to Embassy with John Campbell White promoted to Ambassador Extraordinary and Plenipotentiary.

evidence.quote: John Campbell White, Envoy Extraordinary and Minister Plenipotentiary. Appointed: 1940-11-29. Credentials presented: 1941-03-14. Promoted to Ambassador Extraordinary and Plenipotentiary 1943-04-14.

merged_events.event_description: Normal diplomatic relations interrupted; U.S. ambassador left post and was recalled by Haiti, ending formal representation.

evidence.quote: Normal relations interrupted on 1963-05-15. Note: Commissioned during a recess of the Senate; recommissioned on January 30, 1962, after confirmation. Relations had not yet resumed when Thurston left post on May 26, 1963. On June 14, 1963 the Government of Haiti requested Thurston's recall, and he did not return to post.

merged_events.event_description: Embassy re-established with the presentation of credentials by Ambassador Benson Ellison Lane Timmons.

evidence.quote: Benson Ellison Lane Timmons, Ambassador Extraordinary and Plenipotentiary. Appointed: 1963-11-30. Credentials presented: 1964-01-16. Left post on 1967-05-28.

matched.notes: Exact match on date (1862-10-01) and status (Legation). RDCR confirms establishment of diplomatic relations and American Legation.

CSV date matches RDCR (Event 1: 1943-03-23). POCOM (Event 4) gives 1943-04-14, which is the ambassador's promotion date (personnel event), not the institutional elevation date. Per source priority, RDCR's institutional date is correct.

missing_from_csv.event_description: Normal diplomatic relations with Haiti were interrupted. The U.S. ambassador left post and the Government of Haiti requested his recall, ending formal representation.
missing_from_csv.notes: CITATION ERROR flagged on this event — treat with extra skepticism. However, the underlying historical event (1963 rupture in U.S.-Haiti relations under Duvalier) is well-documented. The exact date of 1963-05-15 should be verified against additional sources before adding to CSV.

missing_from_csv.event_description: Embassy re-established with the presentation of credentials by Ambassador Benson Ellison Lane Timmons III.
missing_from_csv.notes: From POCOM only. This represents the resumption of normal diplomatic relations after the 1963 interruption. If Event 5 is added as a 'None' status, this event should also be added to reflect the restoration of Embassy status.
